title: Request to exclude a single conversation from retention reporting (LUM-83)
preview:
From: Sarah Wong <sarah.wong@lumenhealth.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: security@lumenhealth.com
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: remove / exclude a single conversation from retention reporting - LUM-83

Hi Alex,

We discovered that a sensitive thread containing patient-identifiable PII was inadvertently retained in your audit/retention reporting for one of our integrations. Per our contract and data residency policy, we need Redwood to remove/omit that single conversation ID from all analytics/retention exports and confirm the removal for our compliance audit.

Details: 
- Account: Lumen Health
- Conversation ID (vendor side): LUM-83
- Approximate date of original conversation: 2027-02-18

Can you confirm whether Redwood can:
1) Exclude that conversation from any aggregated reporting/exports and from retention indexes going forward,
2) Provide a short redaction report showing where the item was found and what was removed, and
3) Confirm the action in writing for our auditors.

We'd like to close this by EOD Wed PT if possible. Happy to sign any attestation or DPA addendums you require.

Thanks,
Sarah
Sarah Wong
Senior InfoSec Program Manager
Lumen Health
Attachment: sample-thread-id.txt (text/plain)


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Wong <sarah.wong@lumenhealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 09:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: remove / exclude a single conversation from retention reporting - LUM-83

Hi Sarah — thanks for the heads up and the details. Looping in Lauren (CS) and Ben (Legal/Privacy) so we can coordinate the operational and compliance path. Quick clarifying Qs before we proceed:

1) Is the request limited to: analytics/retention exports and redaction from our audit indices, or do you also want the underlying storage object deleted from all backups and S3 buckets?
2) Do you have a signed legal hold or other retention exception that conflicts with deletion?

Operationally we can run a targeted redaction job against conversation ID LUM-83 and produce a CSV showing the log locations where we removed entries. Ben will advise on required attestation language. If you prefer, we can send a DocuSign attestation for signature to expedite.

I can target a completion estimate of 48 hours after confirmation of scope.

— Alex
Alex Martinez
Support Lead, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai
Attachment: internal-ticket-CASE-2027-4472 (ticket stub)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Wong <sarah.wong@lumenhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: remove / exclude a single conversation from retention reporting - LUM-83

Thanks Alex / Sarah. Legal/Privacy here — short answer: yes, we can perform a targeted exclusion from reporting and produce a redaction report. A couple of items so we stay audit-safe: 

• Scope confirmation: Unless Lumen asks for full media/storage object deletion (including historical backups), we will perform an omission from analytics/retention indexes and remove any derived logs that feed reporting. Please confirm if full object deletion is required.
• Attestation: We need a signed attestation from the Lumen primary contact authorizing the operation and confirming no legal hold exists. I can draft a one-paragraph attestation and circulate via DocuSign.
• Timeline: Once we have signed attestation and conversation ID confirmed, engineering will schedule a redaction job in the next business window. Estimated completion: 24–48 hours, followed by a generated redaction_report CSV and a confirmation email.

Alex — please create CASE-2027-4472 and set priority to High. Lauren — please coordinate engineering window and deliverable formatting for the auditors. Sarah — if you can reply with either 1) confirmation that the scope above is sufficient, or 2) explicitly request full deletion from backups, we’ll proceed accordingly.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Sr. Counsel, Privacy & Security
Redwood Inference


From: Sarah Wong <sarah.wong@lumenhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: security@lumenhealth.com
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 08:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: remove / exclude a single conversation from retention reporting - LUM-83

Ben / Alex — thanks. Lumen confirms scope: exclusion from analytics/retention indexes and removal from reporting exports is sufficient for our auditors; we do NOT require destruction from historical immutable backups at this time. We will sign the attestation. Please send the DocuSign link.

Sarah Wong
Senior InfoSec Program Manager, Lumen Health
Attachment: signed-POC-stub (PDF)

--- Forwarded message from DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/remote-sign/abc123-lum83 ---
DocuSign envelope completed: docusign-lumen-LUM-83.pdf


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Wong <sarah.wong@lumenhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: remove / exclude a single conversation from retention reporting - LUM-83

Sarah — quick update and closure:

1) We received the signed DocuSign attestation and attached it to CASE-2027-4472 (see link).
2) Engineering ran a targeted redaction job against conversation ID LUM-83 during the 2027-03-31 maintenance window. The job omitted the conversation from analytics/retention indexes and removed derived reporting entries; backups were not altered per your confirmation.
3) Attached is the redaction report: redaction_report_LUM-83.csv — it lists the tables/indices and the timestamped actions.

Final confirmation: the conversation LUM-83 has been excluded from reporting exports and retention indexes. We are preserving an internal audit trail of the redaction action (as required by our policy) and have attached the auditor-friendly report.

If you need an expanded report format or a signed cover letter for your auditors, let us know and Ben will prepare it.

Thanks for working through this quickly — closing CASE-2027-4472 now.

— Alex
Alex Martinez
Support Lead, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redaction_report_LUM-83.csv (text/csv, 14KB); docusign-lumen-LUM-83.pdf (application/pdf, 56KB)

title: SOC 2 Type II coverage request — AtlasPay procurement
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Lauren Bell <lauren.bell@atlaspay.com>
Date: 2027-03-28T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: SOC 2 Type II coverage request — AtlasPay procurement

Hi Lauren,

Thanks for running the procurement intake. Attached is a one-page summary of our most recent SOC 2 Type II posture (summary attached). We do have a Type II examination in progress and expect the final report to land in mid-April, but I understand AtlasPay's procurement cutoff is tight.

Can you confirm the exact audit period AtlasPay needs covered and any format preference for an interim/coverage communication (PDF letter, signed attestation, or a bridge-style commitment)? Also please confirm your absolute deadline — you mentioned end of month at the demo but I want to be sure.

If helpful, I can loop in our security and legal immediately. We want to make this as fast as possible to avoid blocking the deal.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai
(415) 555-0183

Attachments: 2027_SOC2_TypeII_summary.pdf (summary, 2 pages)

From: Lauren Bell <lauren.bell@atlaspay.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@atlaspay.com
Date: 2027-03-28T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SOC 2 Type II coverage request — AtlasPay procurement

Alex —

Thanks for the quick reply. Procurement needs evidence that AtlasPay will be covered for controls across the period Oct 1, 2026 — Mar 31, 2027. Our SCO requires a formal Type II report for that period or, if the final report isn't available by our deadline, an interim coverage letter signed by the vendor with a target date for completion of the Type II and contact info for the auditor.

Deadline: 2027-03-31 23:59 PT (we're doing a sign-off run on Thursday). We also need:
- Audit firm name and auditor contact
- Scope statement (in-scope systems and services)
- Any compensating controls in place now if there's a timing gap

Can you get something over by EOD Tuesday (3/29)? That will let procurement include it in the packet.

Appreciate it — happy to hop on a 15m call if that helps.

Lauren Bell
Head of Procurement, AtlasPay
lauren.bell@atlaspay.com
+1 212-555-0134

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-03-28T10:25:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: SOC 2 Type II coverage request — AtlasPay procurement

Marissa — forwarding AtlasPay's note below. They require coverage for Oct 1, 2026 — Mar 31, 2027 and have an EOD 3/31 procurement deadline. Deal ID DEAL-8921, upsell/expansion path, demo scheduled next week.

Can Security produce a signed \"Coverage Commitment\"/interim attestation and auditor contact by EOD 3/29? Procurement wants a signed PDF (auditor + scope + target final report delivery date). Vivek/Sean: what's realistic for us here and do we need Legal to sign off on wording before sending?

I can also offer AtlasPay a read-only link to the audit artifacts folder while we finalize documents if that speeds approval.

Quick answers appreciated — this is high priority.

— Alex
AE, Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-03-28T12:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: SOC 2 Type II coverage request — AtlasPay procurement

Alex — thanks for the heads up. Vivek: can Security commit to producing a signed coverage letter by EOD 3/29? If Legal needs to touch it, we need a one-hour turnaround window.

If we can do that, I want a single-line sales-friendly statement we can use after the letter is signed that confirms the timeline and doesn't overpromise. Procurement is strict on that deadline so please escalate accordingly.

Marissa Cole
Director, Enterprise Sales
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-03-28T16:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: SOC 2 Type II coverage request — AtlasPay procurement

Team — we can produce a Coverage Commitment Letter that meets procurement's ask. Summary of what Security can deliver by 3/29 EOD PT:

- A signed Coverage Commitment Letter (PDF) describing: current SOC 2 Type II in-progress, explicit audit period coverage requested (Oct 1, 2026 — Mar 31, 2027), and our commitment to deliver the final Type II report by 2027-04-15.
- Auditor information: KPMG LLP, Lead Auditor: Jordan Meyers, jordan.meyers@kpmg.com, +1 650-555-9990.
- Scope statement (in-scope components): Redwood Inference API (hosting, model serving), console (RBAC), logging/audit pipeline, and customer data handling subsystems.
- If the final report is delayed, we will include a summary of compensating controls currently in place (detailed control owners included) and offer a secure, read-only folder with supporting artifacts for procurement review.

I've attached a draft Coverage Commitment Letter for internal review: coverage_commitment_letter_draft.docx. Sean, please confirm there's nothing in the wording that would materially conflict with our existing attestation or compliance posture. Alex / Marissa: if this looks good, we will route to Legal for signature and return the signed PDF to you and the prospect.

Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Security Operations
vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai

Attachments: coverage_commitment_letter_draft.docx

title: Hedgewell discovery — next steps & proposed onramp
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Lena Park <lena.park@hedgewell.com>, Mark Dunn <mark.dunn@hedgewell.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Mar 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Hedgewell discovery — next steps & proposed onramp

Hi Lena / Mark —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap from our discovery call and a proposed lightweight onramp so everyone is aligned on next steps. I kept this intentionally short.

Key pains you called out:
- Latency spikes on peak inference windows (search/QA flows) causing customer-facing errors.
- High per-token costs with current multi-tenant setup; need predictable unit economics for pilot.
- Compliance: certain datasets need EU residency; you also mentioned stricter audit/log retention for financial workloads.

Primary goals for the pilot:
1) Reduce per-token inference cost by ~3x relative to current spend for the same quality profile.
2) Maintain P95 latency under 300ms for typical requests and stable behavior during bursts.
3) End-to-end integration with your SSO + KMS; logs retained for 90 days and exportable for audits.

Stakeholders we captured:
- Product: Lena Park (Primary)
- Engineering lead: Mark Dunn (Integration + SRE)
- Procurement/Legal: procurement@hedgewell.com
- Redwood owners: Ava Chen (AE), Ben Carter (Sales Eng), Monica Patel (Solutions Eng)

Proposed mutual onramp (high-level milestones):
- Milestone 1 — Pilot scope & success criteria agreed (Owners: Lena / Ava) — target date: 2027-04-05
- Milestone 2 — Data access & secure connectivity (VPC peering / SFTP + KMS test) (Owners: Mark / Monica) — target date: 2027-04-19
- Milestone 3 — Pilot run (1–2 weeks) + perf/cost baseline (Owners: Hedgewell / Redwood) — target date: 2027-05-03
- Milestone 4 — Exec review & procurement decision (Owners: Lena / procurement@hedgewell.com / Ava) — target date: 2027-05-17

Attachments: I am attaching a first draft MPA and an initial SLA sketch to make reviews easier: Hedgewell_MPA_v0.1.pdf, SLA-draft-Redwood.pdf.

Immediate asks / CTA:

Subject: Hedgewell discovery — next steps & proposed onramp

Hi Lena / Mark —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap from our discovery call and a proposed lightweight onramp so everyone is aligned on next steps. I kept this intentionally short.

Key pains you called out:
- Latency spikes on peak inference windows (search/QA flows) causing customer-facing errors.
- High per-token costs with current multi-tenant setup; need predictable unit economics for pilot.
- Compliance: certain datasets need EU residency; you also mentioned stricter audit/log retention for financial workloads.

Primary goals for the pilot:
1) Reduce per-token inference cost by ~3x relative to current spend for the same quality profile.
2) Maintain P95 latency under 300ms for typical requests and stable behavior during bursts.
3) End-to-end integration with your SSO + KMS; logs retained for 90 days and exportable for audits.

Stakeholders we captured:
- Product: Lena Park (Primary)
- Engineering lead: Mark Dunn (Integration + SRE)
- Procurement/Legal: procurement@hedgewell.com
- Redwood owners: Ava Chen (AE), Ben Carter (Sales Eng), Monica Patel (Solutions Eng)

Proposed mutual onramp (high-level milestones):
- Milestone 1 — Pilot scope & success criteria agreed (Owners: Lena / Ava) — target date: 2027-04-05
- Milestone 2 — Data access & secure connectivity (VPC peering / SFTP + KMS test) (Owners: Mark / Monica) — target date: 2027-04-19
- Milestone 3 — Pilot run (1–2 weeks) + perf/cost baseline (Owners: Hedgewell / Redwood) — target date: 2027-05-03
- Milestone 4 — Exec review & procurement decision (Owners: Lena / procurement@hedgewell.com / Ava) — target date: 2027-05-17

Attachments: I am attaching a first draft MPA and an initial SLA sketch to make reviews easier: Hedgewell_MPA_v0.1.pdf, SLA-draft-Redwood.pdf.

title: Assessment kit finalization & data safeguards for BrightRetail MAP

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@brightretail.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 19 Mar 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Assessment kit finalization & data safeguards for BrightRetail MAP

Hi Elena —

Following our call, attaching the proposed Mutual Action Plan (MAP) draft and a small sample manifest so your team can sanity-check the formats we expect. Key asks in this email:

- Confirm the subset of test cases you want included in the pilot (we suggested 200 prioritized retail checkout and returns scenarios).
- Confirm retention window for ephemeral promptpacks (we default to 30 days unless you request 14 days).
- Any additional compliance evidence you need before we ship (SOC2 artifact pointers are in the MAP appendix).

Attachments: MAP-BR-2026-11-draft.pdf (draft MAP with milestones), BR_evals_v1_sample_manifest.csv (file list & metadata).

Planned next steps: if you OK the MAP draft, we can run a 2-week onboarding sprint: week 1 — ingest & smoke tests in your sandbox; week 2 — joint QA and acceptance gate. Proposed transfer method is SFTP (we have short-lived credentials) or secure Drive the first time.

Cc'ing Karthik for the rollout cadence and Laura for the retention/ops checklist.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0124


From: Elena Park <elena@brightretail.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 19 Mar 2027 09:48:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Assessment kit finalization & data safeguards for BrightRetail MAP

Ben —

Thanks for the quick follow-up and the attachments. A few notes from evaluation:

- Sample manifest looks good, but we need category-level anonymization on user IDs before anything leaves our systems (we can provide a hashing salt or you can apply a one-way hash; preference is ours to provide).
- Retention: Procurement wants 14 days for ephemeral promptpacks; Ops prefers 30 for troubleshooting. Can we make retention configurable per-package?
- Evidence: SOC2 report is sufficient for initial pilots; for production we will expect a signed AUP appendix.

We’ll confirm the exact list of 200 scenarios by Monday. Procurement (cc'd) will follow up on PO and standard MSAs.

Elena
Elena Park
Head of Product Initiatives — BrightRetail
elena@brightretail.com

To: Elena Park <elena@brightretail.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Subject: Re: Assessment kit finalization & data safeguards for BrightRetail MAP

Thanks,
Karthik

title: Procurement readiness and vendor onboarding for NovaRetail
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Eleanor Shaw <eleanor.shaw@novaretail.com>, Tim O'Leary <tim.oleary@novaretail.com>, procurement@novaretail.com
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Mar 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Procurement readiness & next steps after demo

Hi Eleanor / Tim —

Thanks again for your time today — appreciate the good discussion on latency needs and the volume profile for your personalized recommendations pilot. Quick recap and the asks we discussed: 

- Decision criteria we heard: 99th p99 latency < 350ms for 2k QPS bursts, support for private VPC, and token-cost guardrails.
- Procurement items you flagged: vendor intake form, SOC2 report, standard SOW + PO routing.
- Timeline: you indicated procurement cycle ~3 weeks once vendor packet is complete; finance needs a ballpark PO amount to start approvals.

Action items for us:
1) I attached the vendor intake packet and a template SOW for your procurement team (redwood-vendor-intake.pdf, sow-template.docx).
2) I'll create a short PO estimate (po-estimate.xlsx) based on the pilot profile and share by EOD tomorrow.
3) Aditya (cc) can do a short technical deep-dive for your infra/ops folks — propose Thu/ Fri next week for a 30m call.

Questions for you:
- Who exactly in procurement should we send the completed intake packet to (name + email)?
- Is there an internal PO number format or vendor registration step we should know about?

Thanks — looking forward to keeping momentum.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0172

Attachments: redwood-vendor-intake.pdf (PDF, 3 pages), sow-template.docx (DOCX)

Cc: Tim O'Leary <tim.oleary@novaretail.com>, procurement@novaretail.com

Subject: Re: Procurement readiness & next steps after demo

Thanks Ben — helpful recap. A couple of quick points: 

- Procurement contact is Marisol Vega (marisol.vega@novaretail.com) — copying procurement@novaretail.com for intake.
- We do have a vendor registration portal; procurement will do the initial profile and then ask for SOC2 and PII handling docs.
- Tech review: Thu 25 Mar at 11:00am PT works for our infra lead. Can Aditya and an engineer join?

One ask from Tim: can you provide a single-line estimate for the PO so finance can pre-approve an internal budget on their side? They don't need the detailed spreadsheet yet — just a ballpark number.

title: MAP Onboarding — QuantifyAI Private Deployment: Network/VPN + Key Owners
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Josh Turner <josh.turner@quantifyai.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP Onboarding — QuantifyAI Private Deployment: Network/VPN + Key Owners

Josh — thanks again for the productive demo last week. As discussed, we’re kicking off a short Mutual Action Plan (MAP) to move QuantifyAI from POC to a production private deployment (VPC-connected) on Redwood Private. The goal is a go-live window in Q2; this MAP focuses first on network, VPN, and keys so we can validate connectivity and security controls early.

I’ve attached an initial MAP spreadsheet (MAP-QuantifyAI-draft-v1.xlsx) and a quick topology sketch. High-level requests from our side:

- Confirm your networking owner(s) and VPN point of contact (CIDR ranges, preferred VPN type: IPsec/Transit).
- Confirm IAM/KMS owner(s) and any specific key management policy (HSM/KMS provider, rotation cadence).
- Provide an internal stakeholder roster (names + roles + emails) for technical and procurement coordination.
- Please review the attached draft MAP timeline and mark any hard constraints (e.g., procurement windows, security review dates).

Proposed immediate next steps:
1) Technical kickoff (60 min) week of Mar 22 — attendees: Redwood infra + Quantify infra/security owners.
2) Exchange initial VPN config and test accounts within 5 business days after the kickoff.
3) Security artifacts checklist (SOC2 evidence, data retention, audit logs) — we will start gathering items in parallel.

Can you confirm the right contacts on your side and whether Mar 23 or Mar 24 morning PT works for the technical kickoff?

Thanks — looking forward to partnering on this.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123
Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- MAP-QuantifyAI-draft-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)
- quantify-network-topology-sketch.png (image)


From: Josh Turner <josh.turner@quantifyai.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Cc: Alyssa Chen <alyssa.chen@quantifyai.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@quantifyai.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Mar 2027 08:07:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP Onboarding — QuantifyAI Private Deployment: Network/VPN + Key Owners

Camila — thanks for the note and the MAP. We’re aligned on moving quickly. Our initial roster:

- Alyssa Chen — Infrastructure Lead — alyssa.chen@quantifyai.com (primary network/VPN contact)
- Marcus Lee — Security & Compliance (KMS owner, SOC2 liaison) — marcus.lee@quantifyai.com
- Procurement Team — procurement@quantifyai.com (contracts/PO)

We can do the 60-minute technical kickoff on Tue Mar 23 at 10:00 AM PT. Please send an invite and any pre-reads you want us to review. For VPN: we prefer IPsec site-to-site (vendor: Palo Alto GlobalProtect) and our initial CIDR will be 10.22.0.0/16 — we’ll confirm exact tunnel endpoints during the kickoff. For keys: we manage keys in AWS KMS (account xxxxxxxx) and can provide a wrap key if needed; Marcus will outline rotation policy in the kickoff.

I’ll have procurement review the MAP file and follow up on any billing/contract questions.

—Josh
Josh Turner
Head of Infrastructure, QuantifyAI
josh.turner@quantifyai.com | +1 206-555-0199

title: BrightHealth pilot: throughput sizing + streaming constraints follow-up
PO field linting & weekly staggered payments — SigmaCloud deal
preview:
From: Laura Mills <laura@sigmacloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: AP Team <ap@sigmacloud.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: PO metadata requirements + weekly invoices (SigmaCloud)

Hi Ben,

Quick follow from procurement: we have a pilot PO ready but our AP team requires invoices to be issued weekly for the pilot (rather than monthly) and each invoice needs the following fields present in the PDF metadata and electronic XML: po_reference (<= 12 chars), billing_entity_uuid, subscription_id, and usage_summary_line. Our PO template is attached.

Also important: SigmaCloud prefers Net45, but procurement asked whether Redwood can support Net30 for a tiered early-pay discount. There are two constraints on our side: (1) our ERP only accepts PO numbers that are alphanumeric and max 12 characters, (2) we need the invoice cadence to allow a month-end reconciliation file that aggregates weekly invoices into a single monthly ledger entry.

Can you confirm whether Redwood can:
- accept the PO template (attached) as-is, or whether you need a normalized PO number style
- support weekly invoice issuance with a month-end reconciliation CSV
- offer any early-pay discounts for Net30 vs Net45

If you prefer, loop in billing/finance and legal; happy to include them.

Thanks,
Laura
Laura Mills
Head of Procurement, SigmaCloud
Attachment: SigmaCloud_PO_template.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Laura Mills <laura@sigmacloud.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Mar 2027 13:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: PO metadata requirements + weekly invoices (SigmaCloud)

Laura — thanks for the clear ask and the template. Looping Vanessa (billing ops) and Priya (solutions) to confirm implementation details.

High-level answers:
- We can accept weekly invoices for a pilot program; we typically default to monthly for standard accounts but will enable weekly cadence for Enterprise pilots. That said, weekly invoices require a small operational processing fee (one-time setup + $250/wk) unless we consolidate at month-end for reconciliation.
- We can map the PO fields you listed. Two requests from our side:
  1) PO reference string must be alphanumeric and we prefer a consistent prefix (e.g., SC-######) to make ingestion idempotent. If SigmaCloud cannot change format, we can accept up to 12 chars but will need an example PO to validate parsing.
  2) Include subscription_id in the invoice line items (we can accept either UUID or your internal SKU).
- Discount options: for flexibility we can offer a tiered early-pay program: 1.75% if you commit to Net30, 0.75% for Net45. Alternatively, we can do Net45 with a one-time onboarding credit for a 12-month commitment.

Next steps I propose:
1) You share a sample PO (real example) so Priya can validate field parsing.

title: Invoice routing and multi-entity delivery (SFTP vs email / portal)
preview:
From: Tara Nguyen <finance@acmecorp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Acme AP <ap@acmecorp.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Invoice delivery for Redwood account - multi-entity setup

Ben,

We are ready to connect Redwood for invoicing. A few items for confirmation before we go-live:

- We have 4 legal entities across EMEA and NA. Need separate invoices per legal entity (different VAT numbers).
- Preferred delivery: automatic push to our SFTP collector (hosted by us). If not possible, we need email copies to ap@acmecorp.com and portal access for downloading historical invoices.
- VAT evidence: we require the invoice PDF to include the VAT registration number and a copy of any VAT proof attached where applicable.
- Can you confirm invoice filenames and line item structure? Our AP system expects invoice PDF + optional XML for automated ingest.

I've attached the PO for the first month. Let us know the timeline and if you need SFTP credentials or a test account.

Thanks,
Tara Nguyen
Finance Manager, Acme Corp
T: +1 415 555 0142

Attachment: Po_78923.pdf (application/pdf)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Tara Nguyen <finance@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Mar 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice delivery for Redwood account - multi-entity setup

Hi Tara — thanks for the clear summary and the PO. Good news: we support both delivery methods and can do a hybrid fallback. High level options:

1) SFTP push (preferred for your flow)
   - We can push encrypted PDF + optional XML payload for each invoice. Filenames can be: ACME_<entity>_INV_<YYYYMMDD>_<invoiceid>.pdf
   - We support per-entity VAT tags in the invoice header and can attach VAT evidence PDFs when provided.

2) Portal enrollment
   - If you opt in to our portal, invoices will be available as PDF + downloadable XML. Portal offers retention and bulk export.

3) Email copies
   - As interim, we can CC ap@acmecorp.com for a copy of each PDF. This is less reliable for automated ingest but works as fallback.

Next steps I propose:
- Connor (cc'd) to confirm if we can provision an SFTP collector pointing to your host and whether we need public-key auth or username/password.
- Laura to confirm the VAT evidence requirements and whether a separate attached PDF per invoice is sufficient.
- Tara: confirm which entities need separate invoices and whether your AP needs the XML schema included.

I have attached our e-invoice spec and a sample filename convention. If SFTP works, aim for a 2-week window to test + produce the first batch. If not, we can enable portal access and turn on email copies.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.ai | +1 415 555 0220

Attachments: redwood-einvoice-spec.pdf (application/pdf)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>

Date: Thu, 18 Mar 2027 11:10:00 -0700

Can you also confirm the expected lead time to provision a one-way push and the rollback plan to portal/email if there are connectivity issues? Please create a short checklist we can share with Acme.

- Laura: confirm what format we should attach VAT evidence in the invoice bundle. Is a single attached PDF per invoice OK, or do you prefer embedding VAT IDs in the XML? Also let me know any EU reverse-charge flags or cross-border annotations we need to include.

If either SFTP or portal will take longer than 10 business days, let's set up email CC copies as a temporary measure so their AP can start reconciling.

Thanks for the details. Quick ops notes and plan:

- We can do a push to an Acme-managed SFTP endpoint. For a standard one-way push we need: host, port, preferred auth (pubkey or username/password), target directory path, and a test account. If they prefer pubkey, send the public key file and we will provision a temporary user.
- Lead time: 3 business days to stand up the push once credentials are provided, then 2 business days for an internal test run. Fallback to portal/email is straightforward and can be enabled same day.
- Filename and payload: we will support ACME_<entity>_INV_<YYYYMMDD>_<invoiceid>.pdf and an optional ACME_<entity>_INV_<YYYYMMDD>_<invoiceid>.xml for the machine-readable payload.
- VAT evidence: Laura, per our compliance requirements we will attach a VAT-evidence PDF when provided. We can also include VAT fields in the XML.

Action items:
1) Tara / Acme AP: provide SFTP details or indicate portal opt-in.
2) Ben: I will prepare the provisioning checklist and a one-time test run plan.
3) Laura: please confirm the VAT-evidence filename pattern we should expect so we can match attachments to invoices.

I have attached our internal SFTP checklist for quick reference.

Connor O'Brien
Platform Ops Engineer, Redwood Inference
connor_obrien@redwood.ai | +1 415 555 0333

title: Baseline data horizon / what we keep by default for requests, prompts, outputs
preview:
From: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@greenlinehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: dataprivacy@greenlinehealth.com
Date: Mon, 15 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick question on default capture window for hosted API

Hi Ben,

We're doing a quick compliance pass ahead of a pilot and I need a crisp answer: for Redwood's hosted API, what is kept by default when a user sends a request? Specifically: raw user inputs (prompts), model-generated outputs, and any intermediate request/response logs — what's the baseline retention window, and where is the data resident by default for US customers?

Also: do you provide a config to disable storing generated outputs entirely for the pilot? If so, what does that change look like on the retention horizon and export/erase paths?

Appreciate a plain-language summary and any links or attachments we can hand to our privacy team.

Thanks,
Alex Moreno
Head of Product, Greenline Health
alex.moreno@greenlinehealth.com
Attachment: none


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@greenlinehealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Mar 2027 08:46:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick question on default capture window for hosted API

Hi Alex — thanks for flagging this. Short answer up front, then a few details and next steps.

Short answer: for Redwood hosted accounts in the US we keep request-level records (raw inputs + request metadata) for 60 days by default. Model-generated outputs (final responses) are retained for 180 days by default as part of our artifact store used for quality monitoring and optional model ops. Customers can opt into shorter horizons or disable retention of generated outputs for the hosted product via a settings toggle + signed addendum; requests (raw inputs) are treated differently because they power billing/diagnostics and are retained for the baseline 60-day window unless explicitly excluded via a contract addendum.

Details:
- Prompts/raw inputs: 60 days default (US).
- Model outputs (final responses): 180 days default.
- Intermediate logs / request metadata (latency traces, token counts): retained 60 days and aggregated metrics retained longer in anonymized form.
- Residency: default hosted US accounts store data in US regions. EU accounts default to EU residency.

You asked about disabling storage of generated outputs: yes — for pilots we can enable an "outputs-not-stored" config for your tenant. That sets generated outputs to not be persisted to the artifact store; ephemeral streaming buffers are still transiently processed for inference but discarded immediately and do not appear in the 180-day store. Note: disabling outputs persistence removes us ability to run certain quality regressions tied to those artifacts and may change the support/SLAs available.

I'm looping Kim (Security & Compliance) for the exact wording we should pass to your privacy team and to attach the DPA summary / retention matrix.

Next steps: Kim, can you confirm the exact minimal language for the privacy team and attach the retention matrix + DPA summary? Alex — if you confirm you'd like the outputs-disabled pilot, I can open a HubSpot opportunity and our Ops team will prepare the config + DPA route.

Best,
Ben
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: retention_matrix.xlsx (placeholder)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@greenlinehealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Mar 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick question on default capture window for hosted API

Hi Alex — hi Ben,

Adding the formal, minimal language you can hand to your privacy folks. I keep it deliberately short so it can be dropped into an internal memo or vendor inventory.

1) Default hosted capture policy (US accounts):
- Raw user inputs (prompts) and request metadata: retained for 60 days by default. Retention is counted from the time of request ingestion.
- Model-generated outputs (final responses): retained for 180 days by default; used for debugging, quality monitoring, and platform analytics.
- Aggregated operational metrics (no PII): retained beyond those windows for trend analysis; individual-level traces expire on the same schedule as requests.

2) Residency:
- Default: data for an account is stored in the cloud region that corresponds to the account's selected region at signup (US vs EU vs APAC). We offer EU-only hosting for EU accounts.

3) Opt-outs and contractual controls:
- Outputs-not-stored option: Redwood can configure a tenant-level setting that prevents final responses from being persisted. This is available for pilots and enterprise customers and requires a short addendum.
- Prompt exclusion: erasure of prompts or disabling prompt capture requires an executed DPA amendment and may limit platform diagnostics and fraud/billing capability.

4) Deletion workflow:
- Standard deletion is automatic after the retention window; we provide export/erase evidence on request. For formal erasure requests tied to customer accounts we follow the enterprise erase playbook and can deliver a compliance report.

Attached: a concise retention_matrix.xlsx and a 2-page DPA_Summary.pdf covering the addendum language.

Let me know if you want a one-pager we can share with legal (I can draft).

-Kim
Senior Security & Compliance Manager, Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: DPA_Summary.pdf, retention_matrix.xlsx


From: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@greenlinehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: dataprivacy@greenlinehealth.com, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Mar 2027 14:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick question on default capture window for hosted API

Thanks Ben and Kim — this is exactly what we needed. Two quick clarifications from our privacy lead (cc'd):
1) For the outputs-not-stored mode, can you confirm whether any transient artifacts (e.g., streaming buffers or KV cache traces) could be reconstructed from runtime logs? We need assurance they’re not persisted.
2) For the prompt exclusion path, if we request prompt-erasure after ingestion, what is the SLA for evidence of deletion?

If you can add those two points to the one-pager that would be great; otherwise we can take the DPA addendum and run a redline. I'll approve a pilot config once we have the outputs-not-stored confirmation.

Best,
Alex
Head of Product, Greenline Health
Attachment: none

On Wed, Mar 17, 2027 at 11:03 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Alex — hi Ben,
> 
> Adding the formal, minimal language you can hand to your privacy folks. I keep it deliberately short so it can be dropped into an internal memo or vendor inventory.
> 
> [snip]
> 
> -Kim

title: Exploring higher monthly volume commitment & tier re-banding (Lumenware)
preview:
From: Alex Morgan <amorgan@lumenware.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 14 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Exploring a higher monthly volume commitment + tier re-banding

Hi Ben,

We’ve been tracking production traffic and expect sustained growth over the next 12 months — roughly 2.5x our current monthly tokens by Q4. Procurement is pushing for a committed pricing path (monthly commit) rather than purely burst billing so we can forecast costs. Can you lay out options for a stepped increase (monthly commit bands), how the rate bands would change at higher commits, and any incentives for locking a multi-quarter term?

High level asks:
- 3–6 month ramp plan to +50% then +150% commit
- Rate per 1k tokens for each proposed tier and volume discount thresholds
- Any minimum term / notice requirements and billing cadence options

I attached our internal projection to give some numbers. Happy to jump on a 30m call this week to walk through.

Attaching: lumenware_3yr_projection.xlsx (XLSX)

Thanks,
Alex Morgan
Director of AI Platform
Lumenware
amorgan@lumenware.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Morgan <amorgan@lumenware.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Mar 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Exploring a higher monthly volume commitment + tier re-banding

Hi Alex — thanks for the projection and context. Good news: we can support a few different paths depending on how Lumenware wants to trade cadence vs. price. Drafted three pragmatic options below; I attached a short PDF that summarizes the math.

Option A — Immediate flat uplift (simple)
- Increase baseline commit by 60% starting next billing cycle
- No change to existing tier breakpoints; rate for new baseline priced at current +10% discount on incremental tokens
- 30-day notice to step down; 3-month minimum term on uplift

Option B — Staged staircase (recommended for ramp)
- Q1: +50% commit, Q2: +100% (relative to current), Q4: +150% target
- Each staircase step re-bands rates: higher commit buckets get a progressive unit discount (example attached)
- 6-month minimum across pattern; billing alignment monthly; 10% volume rebate at end of quarter if commit exceeded by 20%+

Option C — Annual commit with capacity cushion (predictable pricing)
- Annual committed tokens (prepaid or monthly invoiced) with burst cushion for +20% monthly overage at reduced overage rate
- 12-month term, early termination fee applies

Next steps: if any of these maps to your procurement constraints we’ll deliver a clean amendment and mark-up for DocuSign. Can we do a 30m call Wednesday 3/17 at 11am ET?

Attaching: redwood_commit_options.pdf (PDF)

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales\Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Alex Morgan <amorgan@lumenware.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Mar 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Exploring a higher monthly volume commitment + tier re-banding

Alex / Ben — adding the pricing specifics and a couple of legal/ops constraints so procurement can scope the amendment.

Pricing (example bands, per 1k tokens):
- Tier 1 (0–5M/mo): $2.90
- Tier 2 (5M–20M/mo): $2.35
- Tier 3 (20M+ /mo): $1.95
- Stair-step uplift (Option B) adds a 5–12% discount on incremental volume depending on the committed band

Commercial notes:
- Minimum commitment term for stepped plans: 6 months. For annual commits: 12 months.
- Billing cadence: monthly invoiced; prepay option available for an extra 3% discount.
- Burst cushion: configurable; we typically recommend a 15–25% cushion for early scale.
- Rebate mechanics: quarterly reconciliation; rebates applied as credit on following invoice.

Compliance / procurement asks we’ll need before signing:
- SOC 2 summary and data retention spec (we’ll include SOC2_summary.pdf)
- Standard security addendum (SaaS DPA); signature required from your legal team

If Option B looks good I’ll produce a redlineable amendment (v1) and route for signatures.

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Sr. Commercial Lead — Platform
marissa.cole@redwood.com


From: Alex Morgan <amorgan@lumenware.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Mar 2027 14:37:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Exploring a higher monthly volume commitment + tier re-banding

Thanks — Option B (staircase) aligns best with our run rate and procurement's preference for gradual commit increases. A couple of notes from procurement and our security team: 

- We want the first step (+50%) to take effect on 2027-05-01, with step two on 2027-08-01.
- SOC2 summary and DPA look fine at a glance; security requests a short answers table around data segregation and KV cache retention (can your security team provide a one-page Q&A?)
- Procurement prefers monthly invoicing aligned to calendar month.

If you can send a redlineable amendment and an initial DocuSign envelope with the staircase schedule and the rebate mechanics I can kick this to legal. We aim to have signature before 2027-04-20 to meet budget planning.

On Wed, Mar 16, 2027 at 11:20 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> Alex / Ben — adding the pricing specifics and a couple of legal/ops constraints so procurement can scope the amendment.

Attaching: SOC2_summary.pdf (PDF)

Cheers,
Alex Morgan


From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Alex Morgan <amorgan@lumenware.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Mar 2027 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Exploring a higher monthly volume commitment + tier re-banding

Alex — looping back with the redlineable amendment (v2) and a DocuSign starter. Per the schedule: first stair (50%) effective 2027-05-01; second stair (100% over baseline) effective 2027-08-01; final target (150%) is a planning target for Q4 and will be subject to reconciliation.

Attachments: redwood_amendment_v2.docx (DOCX), docusign_proposal.pdf (PDF)

Links: https://hubspot.example.com/deals/DW-2027-042 (internal deal)

Ben / Marissa — I set the DocuSign envelope to route to Alex -> Lumenware legal -> Redwood finance -> Redwood legal. Alex, let me know if procurement needs any changes to the routing or if they want a consolidated annual invoice instead of monthly.

Connor O'Brien
Customer Success Manager
connor.obrien@redwood.com

title: Bluestone Health — model fit handoff & staging instructions
Aligning demo to CTO arrival window — reschedule request
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>

Subject: Demo follow-up + proposed times for next week

Thanks again for the time today — great conversation. As discussed, we'd like to run a tailored technical demo that covers inference economics, predictable latency for your batch/real-time mix, and the Dedicated deployment options you asked about.

Proposed slots (PT):
- Tue, Mar 23 — 10:00–11:00

title: Renewal negotiation: tiered surge credit + temporary SLA latency hold
preview:
From: Sam Patel <sam.patel@blueharborlogistics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 05 Mar 2027 10:14:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal conversation - cost and burst needs

Ben,

Quick note as we start planning our renewal: our usage profile changed a lot in Q4 — steady baseline but frequent short surges during peak routing windows. We need to reduce unit cost and preserve the ability to burst for short windows. Can Redwood offer a renewal that balances a committed discount with a predictable burst capacity? Procurement also asked about any SLA credits tied to latency spikes.

We're aiming to finalize terms by mid-March. Happy to jump on a 30-min call if that helps.

Thanks,
Sam Patel
Head of Platform Engineering
Blue Harbor Logistics

P.S. attaching our expected monthly token curve (will send file separately).

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@blueharborlogistics.com>
Cc: Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 07 Mar 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal conversation - cost and burst needs

Sam — really appreciate the context and the timelines. Short answer: yes, we can structure a renewal that mixes a committed baseline (for a better unit price) plus a guaranteed burst pool and a small buffer of on-demand capacity. Two things I want to call out up front:

1) Pricing mechanics: we typically offer a stepped commitment (12 or 24 months) with a burst pool that is guaranteed for short windows. Customers on the stepped plan get a per-token discount on committed usage and a lower burst fee than pure on-demand.

2) SLA credits: we can include a temporary SLA latency hold — essentially a pricing credit if latency exceeds the target during covered bursts — but the credit calculation needs to be tightly defined so it isn't ambiguous.

I'll draft a concrete option set and loop in finance/legal for wording. Would you prefer a 12- or 24-month commit? If you expect continued growth, 24 months unlocks a deeper discount.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mobile: (415) 555-0189

--
Attachment: None in this message.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 09 Mar 2027 08:43:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: Blue Harbor renewal — proposed concession structure (need approval)

Team — copying the renewal thread with Blue Harbor. They've asked for a hybrid structure: committed baseline + guaranteed burst pool + an SLA-linked credit for latency during bursts. Summary of what I'm planning to offer (high level):

- Baseline commit: 12 months at 30% off hosted list rate for committed token volume (customer will expand committed volume by ~25% relative to current committed avg).
- Burst pool: guaranteed pool sized for expected peak windows (they'll pay a smaller recurring fee to reserve pool capacity; usage inside pool billed at committed unit rate, usage beyond pool billed at 1.5x committed rate).
- Temporary SLA latency hold: for the first 90 days post-renewal we propose a latency credit if 95th percentile latency during covered bursts exceeds 350ms; credit capped at 5% of monthly invoice and calculated pro-rata per incident.
- True-up: standard calendar-quarter true-up for overages; one free true-up month in year one to smooth migration.

Asks: 
- Mina: can CS/Finance sign off on the 5% credit cap and the 90-day temporary window?
- Marcus: legal needs to confirm the credit language and an amendment approach (we want to avoid a long-term SLA commitment here; keep it scoped to the 90-day stabilization).
- Grace: need your approval on giving a stepped discount of 30% for 12 months (or 38% for 24 months) — this is moderately aggressive.

I've attached the usage heatmap (operational-sla-history-2026.csv) and a starter redline doc. I plan to send the formal proposal to Sam Wednesday if we agree.

Thanks — Ben

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Mar 2027 11:17:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Internal: Blue Harbor renewal — proposed concession structure (need approval)

Ben — quick read from legal: the structure is reasonable with two suggested edits:

1) Replace the vague incident language with a simple metric calculation: credit triggers when 95th percentile P90 latency for requests routed through the committed/burst pool exceeds 350ms measured across a single calendar day; credit equals min(5% of that daily prorated invoice, $20k). This gives a hard dollar cap and a measurement window.

2) Instead of a free true-up month, propose a <30-day> forgiveness window for first-quarter overages to avoid perpetual extensions.

Mina: if finance is comfortable with the $20k cap and the 5% monthly cap, I can draft clean amendment wording. Grace — flag if the discount levels need to be pulled back.

— Marcus

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@blueharborlogistics.com>
Cc: Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 Mar 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal attached: Renewal options and burst safeguard (Blue Harbor)

Sam — attaching the formal proposal and a short one-page summary of options:

Option A (12-month stepped commit): 30% off committed tokens, guaranteed burst pool sized for expected peak windows, on-demand beyond pool at 1.5x committed rate. Temporary SLA latency credit for first 90 days: triggers if 95th percentile latency during covered bursts > 350ms; credit capped at 5% monthly invoice or $20,000, whichever is lower. One-time forgiveness window for first-quarter overage.

Option B (24-month commit): 38% off committed tokens and slightly larger baseline capacity. Burst pool reserved at lower recurring fee. SLA credit same as Option A.

Operational notes: we included an addendum describing how the burst pool is reserved and billed, a simple true-up cadence, and an appendix with the last 12 months of latency and usage (operational-sla-history-2026.csv).

Next steps if you like either option: 1) procurement signature on the attached amendment; 2) DocuSign for the commitment; 3) opt into the reserved burst pool and schedule a cutover week for KV cache priming.

Attachments: redwood-proposal-2027-03-14.pdf, operational-sla-history-2026.csv, amendment-redlines-BlueHarbor.docx

Look forward to your feedback — happy to hop on a call to walk through numbers.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
--
Attachment: redwood-proposal-2027-03-14.pdf

From: Sam Patel <sam.patel@blueharborlogistics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@blueharborlogistics.com
Date: Tue, 16 Mar 2027 17:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposal attached: Renewal options and burst safeguard (Blue Harbor)

Thanks Ben — appreciate the clear options and the appendix. Procurement and I reviewed Option A and have three small asks before we sign: 

1) Can the SLA credit measurement window be daily as you proposed, but with a 48-hour aggregation buffer so transient network blips don't immediately trigger credits?
2) Can we get a 30-day notice on when the burst pool size will change (so ops can coordinate their routing)?
3) Minor legal redline: include an explicit clause that the 90-day temporary SLA credit window can be extended by mutual written agreement if we both agree on an additional stabilization period.

If you can accept the three asks, procurement is ready to route the amendment for signature this week. We prefer Option A to keep flexibility in year one.

Sam
Head of Platform Engineering
Blue Harbor Logistics

title: Novum onramp: scope, buyer roles & security kickoff timeline
Post-call: next steps & technical slot for dedicated capacity sizing (Vertex Labs)
preview:
From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
To: Alex Brown <alex.brown@vertexlabs.com>; Maya Rao <maya@vertexlabs.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Mar 2027 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call: next steps & technical slot for dedicated capacity sizing (Vertex Labs)

Alex / Maya —

Thanks again for the call earlier — good to meet the team and hear about the inference use cases you have for smart document search and real-time reranking. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum: 

Summary of what we heard (short):
- Primary workloads: document search (low-latency top-k reranking) + chat-like summarization for agents.
- Expected steady-state QPS: ~120 qps at peak hours, with up to 600 qps bursts during batch ingest windows.
- Avg token length: 180 tokens for rerank context, 800 tokens for some summarization requests.
- Latency target: 95th <= 200ms for reranking path, 99th <= 600ms for summarization.

Next steps (proposed):
1) Schedule a 60–75 minute technical deep dive focused on workload profile and model selection. We’ll bring Naomi (SRE lead) + Ben (solutions engineer) to map throughput/latency to pool sizes.
2) Run a quick pre-call checklist (attach) to capture concrete QPS/sequence distributions and test corpus.
3) Follow with a reserved dedicated-capacity sizing session where we exercise the model profile and validate KV cache / batching tradeoffs.

Proposed slots (PT):
- Wed 16 Mar, 11:00–12:15 PT
- Wed 16 Mar, 15:00–16:15 PT
- Thu 17 Mar, 09:00–10:15 PT

If you prefer one of these, reply with the best option and we’ll send a calendar invite. I attached my short call notes from today.

Best,
Diego
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: discovery-notes.pdf (PDF, 240 KB)]

--
Diego Fernandez
Redwood Inference
D: +1 415-555-0192

Diego —

Thanks for the clear recap and the notes — that matches our internal read. We’d like to take the 16 Mar slot and prefer the 15:00 PT one.

Couple of quick clarifications from our side before the deep dive:
- We’ll include our infra lead, Samantha Li (samantha.li@vertexlabs.com), on the technical call. Please add her if possible.
- For the rerank path, the 120 qps is our expected sustained load; bursts up to ~600 qps are short (5–10 min) during peak ingestion.
- We run a ~99.9% availability SLO; if you have a recommended fallback model or capacity tier to satisfy that, we’d like to cover it in the session.

We’ll send a calendar invite once you confirm attendees. Also suggest you add any pre-call checklist to HubSpot or as an attachment (we’ll circulate internally).

Thanks — Alex

- 5m quick intros & goals
- 15m workload profile walk-through (you) + Qs
- 30m sizing exercise (Naomi + Ben will lead) — model variants, batching, KV cache assumptions
- 10–20m constraints & rollouts (fallback models, autoscale) + next steps

Pre-call request: please share a small sample request set (5–10 examples) and the expected QPS pattern (minute-level) — we included a short pre-call checklist in the attachment to make it easier. If you can upload those to the HubSpot deal page that’s great; otherwise reply-all with a link.

Please share these items if possible (short bullets are fine):
- Expected 95th and 99th latency targets for each path (rerank vs. summarization)
- Exact QPS distribution (sustained vs. burst windows) in minute granularity if available
- Typical and max sequence lengths for tokens (you mentioned ~180 and ~800) — confirm 95th token length too

- Any security/compliance constraints for a private deployment or key management needs

Also: if you have an SRE on-call or a contact we should include for runbook conversations, drop the name — we’ll cover autoscale policies and safe fallbacks.

Example of short expected answer (pasteable):
- rerank 95th <= 200ms, summarization 99th <= 600ms

- token 95th: 220 tokens (rerank), 950 tokens (summaries)
- model: undecided — would like Redwood recommendations
- compliance: standard cloud; we require dedicated VPC and IAM role separation

If you can drop that in HubSpot or reply-all before 15:00 PT tomorrow, Naomi and Ben will prepare the sizing worksheet and model-run tradeoffs. See you on the call.

Naomi Feldman
SRE Lead | Redwood Inference

title: Mid-cycle microburst: unexplained token surge on Invoice #6372
preview:
From: Oliver Maynard <oliver@seedly.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>
Cc: finance@seedly.com
Date: 2027-03-14T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Invoice #6372 — mid-month surge? Need metering detail

Hi Hannah,

We received invoice #6372 (period Mar 1–15) and there's a ~2.9x jump in "session tokens" for a single integration host on Mar 10. Attached is the invoice and a small sample of our request IDs (usage-sample.csv).

Quick summary:
- Invoice total: $12,314.50
- Chargeline: Runtime tokens — 11,872,320 tokens (vs ~4.1M typical week)
- Host: api-prod-7 (we've confirmed only a single app key used)

Can you provide:
1) Per-request token counts (or a sample) for the host between 2027-03-09 22:00 UTC and 2027-03-11 03:00 UTC
2) Any known batch-rewrite/republish events or ingest replays that could have caused duplication
3) VAT/region tagging for those tokens — we need the breakdown for our reclaim process

We're flagging payment until we get a reconciliation. Not disputing legitimate usage, but this looks anomalous.

Thanks — Oliver
Oliver Maynard
Head of Finance, Seedly Inc.
finance@seedly.com

Attachments: invoice-6372.pdf, usage-sample.csv


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: finance@redwood.com
Date: 2027-03-14T10:01:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Invoice #6372 — mid-month surge? Need metering detail

Ben / Marissa — looping you in. Customer (Seedly) has flagged a concentrated token surge from a single host window on Mar 10. Oliver included call IDs; see attachment.

First pass asks:
- Ben: can you pull per-request token counts / sampling for api-prod-7 for the 2027-03-09 22:00 UTC to 2027-03-11 03:00 UTC window? Include request IDs and prefixes if available. Highlight any duplicated request-ids or large batched streaming responses.
- Marissa: customer is holding payment. If we see an instrumentation/ingest replay issue on our side, what is our typical credit policy for mid-cycle redistributions? Also they asked for VAT region tagging per-token which affects their reclaim.

I'll own comms once we have a quick triage.

— Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
hannah@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: infra-telemetry@redwood.com
Date: 2027-03-14T13:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Invoice #6372 — mid-month surge? Need metering detail

Hannah — I pulled the sample window. Quick findings below (attaching call-ids.txt with matched call IDs).

Summary (api-prod-7, 2027-03-10 00:30–01:45 UTC):
- About ~5.6M tokens in 75 minutes across ~1,240 requests. Typical is ~400–800k.
- 68 request IDs show duplicated ingest prefixes (same request-id reused with new timestamps). Those map to an SDK client library (v1.2.3) that had a known resubmission bug in 1.2.3; we observed identical payload hashes and incremented retry headers.
- A small subset (25 requests) are "batched streaming" mode (client sends 1 call with long multi-chunk input). Our runtime counts all chunks for token billing — if the client retried the same streaming session without KV reuse it will multiply tokens.

Evidence samples (excerpt):
- call_20270310_00_31_112: tokens=12,312 payload-hash=0x8f1a... request-id=abcd-1234
- call_20270310_00_34_007: tokens=48,990 payload-hash=0x8f1a... request-id=abcd-1234 (duplicate id, later timestamp)

Actionable:
1) This looks like a client-side resubmission bug (SDK v1.2.3). We can provide the per-request evidence and suggest a partial credit for duplicated requests.
2) VAT tagging: all those requests are routed via our us-central region; VAT should be zero-rated (US customer). We will still provide the timezone/billing jurisdiction CSV for their accounting.

I'm attaching call-ids.txt (sample matched IDs) and can generate a full per-request CSV for the window if you want me to run it.

-ben
Ben Carter
Billing & Metering Ops, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Maynard <oliver@seedly.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-03-16T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Invoice #6372 — mid-month surge? Need metering detail

Oliver — thanks for flagging this and for the sample. We've completed triage on our side. Short answer: the bulk of the unexpected tokens appear tied to duplicated request-ids from the Seedly SDK (v1.2.3) combined with several long-running streaming calls that were retried. Ben attached the matched call id file for audit.

What we can offer:
- Credit proposal: 55% of the duplicated-request token total for the Mar 10 window (we exclude any legitimately distinct retries). For transparency we will send a line-by-line CSV mapping your provided request IDs to our counted tokens and the credit calculation.
- VAT tagging: since Seedly is US-headquartered and traffic routed via Redwood us-central, the traffic is taxed as US (no EU VAT). We'll produce a per-token jurisdiction CSV for Seedly's reclaim; see Drive link with evidence (https://drive.redwood.com/seedly/evidence/6372).
- Payment hold: propose Seedly release payment for the non-duplicated portion immediately and we apply the credit on the next invoice cycle (or issue a pro-rated credit note now if you prefer).

Next steps: please confirm you want a full per-request CSV export (Ben can run it; will take ~2–3 hrs). Also confirm preference on credit note vs next-cycle offset.

If you want, we can also schedule a short 20-min call with our SDK team to review the retry pattern and mitigation (we can propose a patched client release).

Best,
Marissa Cole
Client Solutions — Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

- Ben: can you pull per-request token counts / sampling for api-prod-7 for the 2027-03-09 22:00 UTC to 2027-03-11 03:00 UTC window? Include request IDs and prefixes if available. Highlight any duplicated request-ids or large batched streaming responses.
- Marissa: customer is holding payment. If we see an instrumentation/ingest replay issue on our side, what is our typical credit policy for mid-cycle redistributions? Also they asked for VAT region tagging per-token which affects their reclaim.

I'll own comms once we have a quick triage.

— Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
hannah@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: infra-telemetry@redwood.com
Date: 2027-03-14T13:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Invoice #6372 — mid-month surge? Need metering detail

Hannah — I pulled the sample window. Quick findings below (attaching call-ids.txt with matched call IDs).

Summary (api-prod-7, 2027-03-10 00:30–01:45 UTC):
- About ~5.6M tokens in 75 minutes across ~1,240 requests. Typical is ~400–800k.
- 68 request IDs show duplicated ingest prefixes (same request-id reused with new timestamps). Those map to an SDK client library (v1.2.3) that had a known resubmission bug in 1.2.3; we observed identical payload hashes and incremented retry headers.
- A small subset (25 requests) are "batched streaming" mode (client sends 1 call with long multi-chunk input). Our runtime counts all chunks for token billing — if the client retried the same streaming session without KV reuse it will multiply tokens.

Evidence samples (excerpt):
- call_20270310_00_31_112: tokens=12,312 payload-hash=0x8f1a... request-id=abcd-1234
- call_20270310_00_34_007: tokens=48,990 payload-hash=0x8f1a... request-id=abcd-1234 (duplicate id, later timestamp)

Actionable:
1) This looks like a client-side resubmission bug (SDK v1.2.3). We can provide the per-request evidence and suggest a partial credit for duplicated requests.
2) VAT tagging: all those requests are routed via our us-central region; VAT should be zero-rated (US customer). We will still provide the timezone/billing jurisdiction CSV for their accounting.

I'm attaching call-ids.txt (sample matched IDs) and can generate a full per-request CSV for the window if you want me to run it.

-ben
Ben Carter
Billing & Metering Ops, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Maynard <oliver@seedly.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-03-16T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Invoice #6372 — mid-month surge? Need metering detail

Oliver — thanks for flagging this and for the sample. We've completed triage on our side. Short answer: the bulk of the unexpected tokens appear tied to duplicated request-ids from the Seedly SDK (v1.2.3) combined with several long-running streaming calls that were retried. Ben attached the matched call id file for audit.

What we can offer:
- Credit proposal: 55% of the duplicated-request token total for the Mar 10 window (we exclude any legitimately distinct retries). For transparency we will send a line-by-line CSV mapping your provided request IDs to our counted tokens and the credit calculation.
- VAT tagging: since Seedly is US-headquartered and traffic routed via Redwood us-central, the traffic is taxed as US (no EU VAT). We'll produce a per-token jurisdiction CSV for Seedly's reclaim; see Drive link with evidence (https://drive.redwood.com/seedly/evidence/6372).
- Payment hold: propose Seedly release payment for the non-duplicated portion immediately and we apply the credit on the next invoice cycle (or issue a pro-rated credit note now if you prefer).

Next steps: please confirm you want a full per-request CSV export (Ben can run it; will take ~2–3 hrs). Also confirm preference on credit note vs next-cycle offset.

If you want, we can also schedule a short 20-min call with our SDK team to review the retry pattern and mitigation (we can propose a patched client release).

Best,
Marissa Cole
Client Solutions — Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

title: Private hosting options & proposed next steps
Demo time confusion around NZ/US DST change — please confirm
preview:
From: Sophie Lloyd <sophie.lloyd@fintechx.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Nguyen <tom@fintechx.com>

Subject: Re: Demo availability — proposed Mar 21 10:00 NZDT

Hi Chloe / Marissa,

Thanks again — we would love to lock in the demo. We were talking internally and the team leaned toward the slot you suggested: Mar 21 at 10:00 NZDT. However, I'm worried there might be a mismatch because of the NZ DST (UTC+13) and the US daylight savings changes earlier this month — I want to make sure our attendees in APAC and the US see the same wall-clock time.

I've attached an .ics with the original slot and a quick CSV I exported from our calendar showing attendee timezones (attached). Can you confirm this maps to the right US times for your presenters? If there's any ambiguity we can pick an alternate window.

Attachments:
- proposed_slots.ics (calendar invite stub)
- slot-conversions.csv (spreadsheet stub)

Thanks,
Sophie

Head of Integrations, FintechX
Sophie.lloyd@fintechx.com

On Sun, Mar 14, 2027 at 08:15 +13:00 Tom Nguyen <tom@fintechx.com> wrote:
> Sophie: 10am NZDT is fine for our folks but double-check with Redwood — I think the US side might be getting tripped by the recent clock change.
>
> -- Tom


From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
To: Sophie Lloyd <sophie.lloyd@fintechx.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 Mar 2027 09:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Demo availability — proposed Mar 21 10:00 NZDT

Hi Sophie — thanks for flagging this. Short answer: we can run the demo at a time that works for both APAC and the US, but there are two different DST transitions in play and they do create ambiguity in some calendar tools.

Quick notes / options:
- NZDT on Mar 21 is UTC+13.
- The US Pacific side has already moved to DST this month (UTC-7). Many calendar clients will display both correctly, but when meeting creators are in different zones some clients (and calendar exports) sometimes show the wrong offset.

To avoid confusion, I recommend either:
1) Confirm the meeting in NZ local time (we host at 09:00 or 10:00 NZDT) and we include explicit UTC times in the invite and the meeting title (e.g., "09:00 NZDT / 18:00 UTC / 11:00 PT").
2) Pick an APAC-first window earlier in the day for NZ and ask US attendees to join late afternoon UTC.

Concrete proposals (all Mar 21):
- Option A (APAC-friendly): 09:00 NZDT / 20:00 AEST / 18:00 UTC / 11:00 PT
- Option B (Middle-ground): 07:00 NZDT / 18:00 AEST / 16:00 UTC / 09:00 PT

I've attached the slot-conversions.csv with the conversions I ran locally — can you confirm which you'd prefer? If you want, I'll create a single calendar invite that includes the UTC string in the title so there's no ambiguity.

Jordan (cc'd) — once Sophie confirms which option works, can you send the calendar invite and include our Zoom link?

Chloe Martin
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
chloe.martin@redwood.ai
Attachment: slot-conversions.csv (conversion table, stub)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sophie Lloyd <sophie.lloyd@fintechx.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Mar 2027 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Demo availability — proposed Mar 21 10:00 NZDT

Thanks Chloe — quick confirm from infra/ops side. I ran the conversions locally and here's a clean table you can paste into the invite: 

- 09:00 NZDT (UTC+13) = 20:00 AEST (UTC+10) = 18:00 UTC = 11:00 PT (UTC-7)
- 10:00 NZDT (UTC+13) = 21:00 AEST = 19:00 UTC = 12:00 PT
- 07:00 NZDT = 18:00 AEST = 16:00 UTC = 09:00 PT

Recommendation: pick 09:00 NZDT (first row) as it keeps APAC in morning and US attendees in mid/late morning PT — less late-night for APAC and not too early for the US west coast. We'll also record the session as a backup for any EU team members who can't join.

If you'd like me to create the invite instead of Jordan I can. Also note: include the literal UTC string in the event title (e.g., "Mar 21 — 09:00 NZDT / 18:00 UTC") to avoid client UI mismatch.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Product Partnerships
Redwood Inference
Attachment: calendar_screenshot.png (screenshot stub showing conversions in my calendar)

Date: Mon, 14 Mar 2027 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Demo availability — confirmed 09:00 NZDT

Perfect — 09:00 NZDT on Mar 21 works for our core group.

Please send the calendar invite with the UTC string in the title and include a Zoom link; we'll accept it from there. If anything changes we'll reply quickly but otherwise see you then.

Thanks and appreciate the quick coordination,
Sophie

>
> Chloe

title: Hosted API performance: P95/P99 skew linked to request queue buildup (PivotalCare)
preview:
From: Alice Reynolds <alice.reynolds@pivotalcare.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Mar 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Hi Ben,

We're seeing a sudden increase in tail latency from your Hosted API for our production tenant. P95 has climbed from ~220ms to ~1.2s and P99 is now consistently 2.5-3s for text.generate calls. This started about 3 hours ago and coincides with a higher error spike (5xx ~0.6%). Throughput is otherwise steady.

We rely on the 99th percentile for user-facing features and need this mitigated quickly. I've attached a short sample of traces and the aggregated P95/P99 export. Can your team confirm whether this is related to batching or request queueing?

Thanks,
Alice Reynolds
Head of Platform Operations
PivotalCare

[Attachment: pivotalcare_p95_p99_run_20270311.csv (text/csv)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alice Reynolds <alice.reynolds@pivotalcare.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Mar 2027 09:03:22 -0700
Subject: Re: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Hi Alice — thanks for the heads up and attachment. We’re treating this as a Sev2 and starting a focused triage.

Immediate notes:
- We’ve seen similar symptoms before when queue depth and batching align poorly with sudden request fan-in: p95 rises while overall throughput is unchanged.
- I’m pulling the request queue snapshots and recent kernel/batching metrics now.
- Naomi is pulling logs for 5xx patterns to see if the errors map to specific model variants or nodes.

Can you confirm whether the affected endpoints include streamed responses or function-calling flows? That changes how we treat KV cache pressure.

Ben
Ben Carter
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

[Attachment: p95_trace_0311.txt (text/plain)]

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Mar 2027 09:21:10 -0700
Subject: Re: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Ben/Marissa — preliminary log correlation: several inference nodes show a sustained queue depth increase starting 11:02Z. Batching sizes concurrently rose by ~2.1x on some routes; autoscaler did not add capacity due to a short-lived spike threshold. No memory OOMs, but KV cache hit ratio dropped ~8% which would increase decode path latency.

I’m attaching the request-queue snapshot and a couple of raw traces that show requests waiting in queue then being batched into large groups. Suggest follow-ups: shorten max batch window for PivotalCare routes and temporarily route them to lower-batch, higher-replica pools.

Naomi

[Attachment: request-queue-snapshot-0311.json]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Mar 2027 10:05:44 -0700
Subject: Re: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Team — quick plan for mitigation while we continue root-cause:
1) Apply immediate rule: reduce max_batch_window_ms from 40 -> 10 for the PivotalCare routing policy.
2) Spin up two extra replicas in the US-hosted pool for the next 60 minutes (manual burst).
3) Enable prefix caching for frequent short prompts (PivotalCare has a high prefix hit candidate set).

Ethan/ops: can you run the rollout? Ben, please communicate the temporary impact and ETA to Alice. I’ll monitor for any increase in 5xxs from the deployment.

Marissa
Director, Platform Ops

On Thu, Mar 11, 2027 at 09:03, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Alice — thanks for the heads up and attachment. We’re treating this as a Sev2 and starting a focused triage.


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alice Reynolds <alice.reynolds@pivotalcare.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Mar 2027 10:22:09 -0700
Subject: Re: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Hi Alice — quick update: we identified a short window where request arrivals triggered larger-than-expected batch coalescing which increased queue hold times for low-latency requests. We’re applying a temporary routing policy: lower batch window + extra replicas. Expected to see P95 drop within ~8-12 minutes after the change.

What to expect: a small increase in cost due to extra replicas and reduced batching efficiency; latency should improve for tail percentiles. We’ll keep you posted and provide the post-mortem once root cause is finalized.

Ben

[Attachment: scheduled-change-notes.txt (text/plain)]

From: Alice Reynolds <alice.reynolds@pivotalcare.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@pivotalcare.com
Date: Fri, 12 Mar 2027 02:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Ben/Team — thanks for the fast turnaround. I can confirm P95 is back down to ~260-280ms and P99 under 1.1s after your changes. We'll expect the post-mortem and any config recommendations for long-term stability (batch tuning, routing policies, recommended autoscaler thresholds).

If you can include a short list of guardrails we can add on our side (client-side concurrency limits / request pacing) that would be helpful.

Appreciate the responsiveness — Alice

On Thu, Mar 11, 2027 at 10:22, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Alice — quick update: we identified a short window where request arrivals triggered larger-than-expected batch coalescing which increased queue hold times for low-latency requests. We’re applying a temporary routing policy: lower batch window + extra replicas. Expected to see P95 drop within ~8-12 minutes after the change.

preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena@nimbushealth.com>
