title: Ancillary security fees, implementation timeline & onboarding milestones
preview:
From: Martin Green <m.green@carebridge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: CareBridge Security Team <security@carebridge.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Ancillary security fees + onboarding timing

Hi Ben,

Quick one from procurement/security as we scope our Redwood evaluation: can you send a line-item breakdown for the ancillary security items we discussed — audit log retention (90d vs 365d), KMS managed key vs HSM seat, data residency surcharge (US regions vs multi-region), and the HIPAA support package. For each item please indicate: (1) whether it is a one-time implementation fee or a recurring monthly/annual charge, (2) the recommended default tier for a 500k monthly token workload, and (3) any onboarding/implementation timeline impact.

We also need an estimate of the total first-year TCO (including onboarding) and what concessions, if any, you typically offer for a 12-month committed Dedicated instance. Our legal team will need a short SOW and an evidence checklist for SOC2/HIPAA.

Can you get something to me by EOD Wednesday? We kick off procurement review next Friday and having these line items will speed things up.

Thanks,
Martin
Martin Green
Head of Cloud Operations | CareBridge Health
m.green@carebridge.com
[Attachment referenced: procurement-template.docx]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Martin Green <m.green@carebridge.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Mar 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Ancillary security fees + onboarding timing

Martin —

Thanks for the clear ask. High level breakdown attached (security-addon-pricing.xlsx) and a draft SOW (CareBridge_SOW_v1.docx). Summary below — I called out which items are typically one-time vs recurring and an expected timeline:  

- Audit logs: Tiered retention. 90 days (default) — $0.02/token-month (recurring storage + retrieval tier); 365 days — additional $0.015/token-month incremental on top of base (recurring). One-time ingest indexing setup: $12k. Typical timeline impact: +1 week for retention tiering and validation.

- KMS vs HSM: Managed KMS (customer-managed keys in Redwood KMS integrations) — recurring: $0.01/token-month for key operations at scale; no onboarding fee. HSM (FIPS/HSM seats) — one-time provisioning & attestation: $35k per HSM seat + $1k/month per seat (recurring). Timeline: HSM provisioning + attestation typically 3–6 weeks (depends on HSM vendor SLA).

- Data residency: Regional residency addon (single region) — one-time configuration & legal attestation: $8k; recurring: $0.03/token-month premium for multi-region high-availability. Timeline: +2–4 weeks for network/VPC peering and routing.

- HIPAA / compliance package: Recurring compliance surcharge to cover audit support, evidence retention, and enhanced SLAs — $4k/month. One-time onboarding for policy mapping and HC-specific controls: $18k. Timeline: 2–3 weeks for initial mapping and attestation prep. Includes SOC2 evidence checklist (attached SOC2-evidence-checklist.pdf).

Pilot/concession options: for a 12-month committed Dedicated commit we can offer: 20% credit on the onboarding fees (one-time items) and a 10% discount on recurring add-on fees for the first 6 months. If you want a larger onboarding credit (up to 30%), we typically require a 24-month commit and a modest uplift on minimum throughput.

Estimated first-year TCO (rough): base inference + addons = $1.45M — includes Dedicated capacity estimate for your expected volume, onboarding fees before credits, and 365d audit retention tier. See attached spreadsheet for line-by-line.

Attachments: security-addon-pricing.xlsx (detailed line items), CareBridge_SOW_v1.docx (draft SOW), SOC2-evidence-checklist.pdf

Let me know if you want me to loop legal (Dev) in for PO/SOW redlines or to schedule a quick 30-min sync to walk through the spreadsheet.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
Office: +1 (415) 555-0134
[Attachment: security-addon-pricing.xlsx (excel)]
[Attachment: CareBridge_SOW_v1.docx (docx)]
[Attachment: SOC2-evidence-checklist.pdf (pdf)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Mar 2027 09:34:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Re: Ancillary security fees + onboarding timing

Folks — quick internal. Forwarding Ben's response with my notes inline.

On Tue, Mar 1, 2027 at 11:05 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Martin —
> 
> Thanks for the clear ask. High level breakdown attached (security-addon-pricing.xlsx) and a draft SOW (CareBridge_SOW_v1.docx). Summary below — I called out which items are typically one-time vs recurring and an expected timeline:  
> ...

My take / negotiation points:  
- Offer: onboarding credit looks reasonable. Recommend we make it 25% onboarding credit for 12-month commit (instead of 20%) but cap total onboarding concession at $40k. Helps close while protecting net.
- HSM: keep the $35k seat, but offer deferred attestation milestone: 50% upfront to cover provisioning, 50% on attestation delivery. Helps procurement cashflow.
- Residency: push for a single-region waiver for first 90 days if they take pilot — lowers their initial barrier.
- HIPAA surcharge: can we structure as a monthly recurring but allow a one-time discount for the first 3 months? Suggest 2 months free or 50% off month 1 if we need to sweeten.

Dev — legal will want SOW redlines and a PO path (Net45/Net30?). Please flag any clauses (export controls, data transfer, indemnity) we should pre-clear.

Hannah — can you confirm CS can commit to a 3-week implementation window for a pilot if CareBridge signs next Friday? If not, we should lengthen timeline expectations in the SOW.

-L
Laura Bennett
Director, Sales Operations | Redwood Inference

[Internal: please see attached security-addon-pricing.xlsx — I've added a concessions column]

To: Martin Green <m.green@carebridge.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Mar 2027 16:45:00 -0700

Martin —

Following up with a refined, procurement-ready package. Attached: updated security-addon-pricing.xlsx (concessions column populated) and an updated SOW ready for signature. Summary of changes from my earlier note:  

- Onboarding credit: 25% credit on one-time onboarding fees for a 12-month Dedicated commit. Total onboarding credit capped at $40,000.
- HSM provisioning: 50% provisioning fee upfront, 50% on attestation delivery (reduces upfront PO).
- Residency pilot: waiving the single-region residency premium for the first 90 days of a pilot (network & routing still required).
- HIPAA package: first month 50% off the recurring compliance surcharge.

Action items for CareBridge:
1) Confirm desired retention tier (90d vs 365d) so we can lock recurring pricing.
2) Confirm desired HSM seat count or opt for managed KMS.
3) Confirm PO cadence (Net30/Net45) and billing contact.

If you prefer, I can schedule a 30-min call tomorrow to walk through the line items with procurement and security.

Best,

Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference

title: Private Offer — 6-month pilot pricing & buyer approval roadmap
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Priya Singh <priya.singh@aurorahealth.com>, Mike Donahue <mike.donahue@aurorahealth.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Private Offer — 6-month pilot pricing & buyer approval roadmap

Hi Priya / Mike,

Thanks again for the demo last week — great conversation on the inference cost/latency constraints for your telemetry ingestion use case. Per our call, attached is an initial private offer structured as a 6-month pilot so Aurora can validate latency at scale without committing to an annual term right away.

High-level summary (attached: PO_Draft_Aurora_6mo.pdf):
- Term: 6 months (starts on activation)
- Billing: monthly invoice tied to AWS Marketplace Private Offer + PO
- Pricing: 35% off our standard marketplace list rate for the selected compute bundle (detailed in the PDF)
- Co-sell credit: 10% (applied as a marketing/payout credit, reflected in partner terms)

Two quick notes about approvals:
1) AWS Private Offers still require the buyer to generate a PO and provide signatures from Procurement + Legal; typical internal reviewers we see at mid-market: Procurement Manager, Legal Counsel, then VP of Cloud/Platform.
2) To accelerate signing we can pre-populate the AWS PO fields and attach a signed offer PDF + DocuSign link.

Enclave boundary snapshot + SOC 2 / SIG evidence request
preview:
From: Sally Pearson <sally.pearson@acme-corp.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: audits@acme-corp.com
Date: Mon, 1 Mar 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: isolation topology and boundary narrative for SOC 2 / SIG

Hi Chloe,

Thanks for taking our SOC 2 / SIG request. As part of our infrastructure assessment for the upcoming audit, we need a short packet describing how Redwood handles network and tenancy isolation for Dedicated and Private deployments. Specifically, could you provide: 

- A high-level network diagram showing control plane vs data plane separation for Dedicated and Private modes (annotated with zones/subnets/VLANs).
- A one-page boundary narrative describing tenant isolation guarantees and egress restrictions.
- A segregation matrix mapping controls to SIG/CAIQ items we flagged (we have a shortlist attached to the HS ticket).
- Any attestations or signed statements you can share about air-gapped/on-prem options and KMS/HSM boundaries.

Our timeline is tight: draft packet by EOD Wed (2027-03-03) would be ideal. Please let me know if that is feasible and who on your side we should expect artifacts from.

Thanks,
Sally
Audit Program Manager, Acme Corp

[Attachment: acme_audit_flags.csv]

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Sally Pearson <sally.pearson@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Mar 2027 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: isolation topology and boundary narrative for SOC 2 / SIG

Hi Sally — thanks for the request and timeline. We can deliver a draft packet by EOD Wed. I am looping in Ben and Kim for the topology + segregation matrix; Jordan will coordinate the packaging and signatures.

Quick notes so we align on scope: 
- Ben will produce an annotated PDF diagram (control vs data plane, egress choke points, edge demarcation).
- Kim will compile the mapping to SIG/CAIQ items and produce the segregation_matrix_acme.xlsx.
- Jordan will add the one-page boundary narrative and package any attestations (we can provide a standard attestation letter for Private deployments; for fully air-gapped on-prem customers we provide a tailored statement).

Ben / Kim / Jordan — can you confirm ETA and whether you need anything else from Acme (e.g., list of services they plan to run on Dedicated vs Private)?

Chloe
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Office: +1 415 555 0123



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 10:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: isolation topology and boundary narrative for SOC 2 / SIG

Chloe — confirming I have the request. Deliverables attached: redwood_enclave_topology_v1.pdf (annotated) and a starter segregation matrix (segregation_matrix_acme.xlsx). Summary below; see attached PDF for visuals.

Summary (technical):
- Deployment modes: Hosted API (shared multi-tenant), Dedicated (reserved GPU pools in customer-designated cloud region), Private (VPC or on-prem with customer-managed networking). This packet focuses on Dedicated and Private.
- Control plane vs Data plane: Control plane (management/orchestration) lives in a hardened control VPC with strict IAM and mTLS between Redwood services; data plane nodes (inference runtime) are provisioned in per-customer subnets for Dedicated and private tenant projects for Private deployments. The PDF annotates the flow.
- Tenant isolation: For Dedicated, we use per-customer VLANs and instance-level tenant tagging; requests are routed through policy gateways that enforce per-tenant prefix/KV cache separation. For Private (VPC), tenant resources are deployed into customer-managed networks with private peering; we do not share runtime hosts across unrelated customers unless explicitly contracted.
- Egress & firewalling: Default deny egress with explicit egress proxies for telemetry and model downloads. Egress to customer endpoints is controlled via customer-managed NAT or dedicated egress nodes for Dedicated.
- KMS/HSM boundary: Customer-managed KMS is supported for Private; Redwood-managed KMS backed by HSM is used for hosted/dedicated when customers opt in. Key usage is logged and mapped in the segregation matrix.
- Attestations: We can provide a standard attestation letter confirming the above controls; Jordan will coordinate signatures.

Attachments included (stubs):
- redwood_enclave_topology_v1.pdf (diagram: control/data plane, subnets, egress, peering)
- segregation_matrix_acme.xlsx (mapping: SIG domains -> Redwood controls; columns: control_id, description, evidence_link)

Drive sources: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1a2b3cEXAMPLE (diagrams + editable source files)

Quoted for context:

> > Hi Sally — thanks for the request and timeline. We can deliver a draft packet by EOD Wed. I am looping in Ben and Kim for the topology + segregation matrix; Jordan will coordinate the packaging and signatures.

Ben
Senior Infrastructure Security Engineer, Redwood Inference

Cc: audits@acme-corp.com
Date: Wed, 3 Mar 2027 11:45:00 -0700

preview:
From: Alice White <alice.white@capitaledge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: security@capitaledge.com
Date: Tue, 02 Mar 2027 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: SIG follow-up — request for event trail evidence and timebound access

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. CapitalEdge is working through the SIG and a couple of items on our roster are specific to event logging and customer access to logs. Can you provide: 

- A short description of how tenant event logs are collected, what fields are recorded (timestamp, request id, model id, prompt hash, user id pseudonymization), and retention defaults.
- A sample export (anonymized) showing a short timeline for a customer incident (no PII).
- The procedure and controls for providing timebound access/export to a customer (audit trail, requester approvals, method of delivery).
- Any SOC2/ISO references or sections relevant to log integrity and tamper detection.

We need this for our internal risk committee and to map the SIG answers to evidence. If possible, can you provide temporary, read-only access to a sanitized S3 export or a presigned link for the sample? Attaching our request template (CapitalEdge_SIG_Request.pdf).

Best,
Alice White
Security & Risk — CapitalEdge



From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Alice White <alice.white@capitaledge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up — request for event trail evidence and timebound access

Hi Alice —

Appreciate the clear ask and the template. Quick summary and proposed next steps below. Full exports and the presigned link will require a short approvals chain on our side (security review + export request log), but we can deliver within 48 hours once approved.

Summary (short):
- Collection: Tenant event logs are captured via our edge routing layer and ingestion pipeline. Events include ISO8601 timestamp, request_id (UUID v4), model_tag (e.g., gpt-rl-13b), route_id, token_count, approximate latency_ms, and truncated prompt_hash (SHA256 first 8 chars). User-identifying fields are pseudonymized at extraction and never included in shared exports unless a legal/data request is authorized.
- Retention: Default retention for customer-facing event streams is 90 days in hot storage; 365 days in cold-tier archival (encrypted). Retention windows are configurable per tenant for Dedicated/Private customers.
- Access & export: We provide timebound, auditable exports via presigned S3 URLs or a one-time secure download bundle stored for 7 days. All exports are logged; the export record includes approver, requester, scope (UTC time window), and a checksum (SHA256) for delivered artifact.
- Integrity: All log files are HMAC-signed at generation and we maintain export checksums and an internal immutable index for tamper-evidence. SOC 2 control references: CC6 (logging and monitoring) and CC7 (system change management) — see attached excerpts.

Next steps we propose:
1) Security review (internal) — Marissa to confirm export scope and redaction rules.
2) Create sanitized sample (Neha) — produce anonymized CSV + checksums.
3) Generate presigned link and send to CapitalEdge with scope + expiry + audit record.

I've looped in Marissa (security lead) and Neha (log exports) to coordinate approvals and the sanitized sample. Is a 48-hour turnaround OK for you? If you need the sample earlier, we can provide a pre-redacted row-level snippet in-line.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Business Development — Redwood Inference

Cc: Alice White <alice.white@capitaledge.com>

- Export window limited to 7 days of events unless explicitly authorized.
- All user identifiers must be pseudonymized; any potential PII redaction must be applied at extraction.
- Exports will include an export manifest with SHA256 checksum and an approval ticket id.

title: SLA credit request — content-filter fallback and shared remediation plan
preview:
From: Samir Patel <samir@finetext.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement <procurement@finetext.ai>
Date: 2027-02-20T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Major latency/SLA hits this morning — request for credit and plan

Ben,

We saw a significant spike in tail latency starting ~08:40 PST today; our customer-facing endpoints backed by the Redwood API reported increased 500/timeout rates and elevated P95/P99. We believe the root cause is the content-filter module invoking downstream moderation tooling redundantly — when the filter path returned an error the runtime retried and amplified latency.

This impacted three production services at Finetext (indexing, assistant, and summarization). Our ops attach a CSV with the request IDs and timestamps. We need: a formal SLA credit for the period of 08:40–09:50 PST and a remediation plan with timelines. Please escalate — this impacts our renewal conversation.

Attachments: filter-incident-log-20270220.csv (CSV, 124KB)

Regards,
Samir Patel
Head of Platform Engineering
Finetext Labs


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@finetext.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-02-20T10:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Major latency/SLA hits this morning — request for credit and plan

Samir — thanks for the flag and the incident log. I’m sorry for the disruption; I’ve looped in Laura (Account Lead) and Karthik (SRE) so we can move quickly.

Initial read: the CSV shows a cluster of timeouts correlated with a spike in moderation-toolbox latency. Short-term options we can put in place today:
- Temporarily reduce filter strictness (fail-open for non-security signals) to stop retries that blow out latency
- Route critical traffic to a degraded model variant with lower tool-calling dependency (cost/quality tradeoff)

I’m attaching a proposed mitigation plan that outlines a 48–72 hour rollback and patch schedule. Before we finalize credit, we need to confirm scope (which endpoints/consumer apps are in-scope) and verify the incident window. Can you confirm the list of affected endpoint names and whether indexed data loss occurred?

I will coordinate a formal credit proposal and an expedited patch timeline once SRE confirms root cause.

Attachment: proposed-mitigation-plan-FT-31415.pdf (PDF, 85KB)

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-02-20T12:05:00-08:00
Subject: Internal: approval for provisional credit and fast-track patch

Ben / Karthik — internal note (do not forward to Finetext until legal & finance sign off).

Summary of recommended customer-facing position: offer an operational credit for the measured SLA window and commit to a firm remediation plan with a date-based milestone. Proposed credit: 10% service credit for February billing applied to Finetext's account (FT-31415). Rationale: the incident appears to be caused by interaction between our filter retry logic and an upstream moderation provider slow-down — we own the overall runtime behavior.

Karthik: can SRE provide a short incident RCA and an ETA for the filter patch (we’re aiming for 48–72 hours)? Finance/Legal: (cc’d out of this thread separately) please confirm the mechanics of applying a one-off credit.

If we can hit the patch and a scheduled maintenance window this week, I’ll authorize Ben to present the credit + remediation commitment to Finetext.

Laura Bennett
Senior Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@finetext.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-02-28T16:40:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Major latency/SLA hits this morning — credit and remediation plan

Samir — thank you for your patience while we validated the incident. Below is what we’re committing to and next steps.

Decision and credit
- Redwood will issue a one-time 10% service credit for the billing period that includes 2027-02-20, applied to your February invoice (FT-31415). Finance will process this within two billing cycles.

Mitigation and timeline
- Root cause (summary): filter retry amplification against a transient moderation-provider slowdown led to cascading retries and elevated P99 latency. RCA doc: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INF-8721
- Immediate action (done): we activated a controlled fail-open for non-security content signals on 2027-02-20 at 11:10 PST which reduced timeouts for critical endpoints.
- Patch schedule: SRE will deploy the filter retry limit and improved backoff on 2027-03-02 during a scheduled maintenance window (02:00–03:00 PST).
- Post-deploy validation: we’ll run a focused synthetic workload and share the runbook/metrics within 24 hours of the deploy.

Shared remediation offer
- Because this impacted your critical production traffic, Redwood will absorb the 10% credit. We also propose a joint post-mortem call and a shared test plan to validate production behavior before your renewal. If Finetext wants an accelerated patch with dedicated engineering support, we can provide a prioritized engagement (SOW/price to be discussed).

Next steps for you
1) Confirm the list of production endpoints you want explicitly included in the credit scope.
2) Provide a convenient window for a post-mortem call (I’ve cc’d Priya from Solutions to coordinate).

Thanks again — we want to make this right and ensure there’s no repeat.

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198

title: Retroactive capacity backcharge — Jan spike and escrow proposal
preview:
From: Kyle Anders <kyle.anders@clearbridge.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Maria Chen <maria.chen@clearbridge.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Feb 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Retroactive capacity charge — Jan spike and renewal

Hi Aditya,

Quick note from procurement after we closed last week's renewal: our finance team flagged a +42% usage spike in January tied to a new ingestion pipeline we flipped on. It looks like that pushed us above our committed capacity for the month and produced an overage that wasn't accounted for in the renewal.

Can you share the line-item reconciled numbers and a proposed one-time charge? Our CFO wants either an amortized settlement across Q1 or a short escrow holdback while we validate logs (we'll need audit evidence).

Attached our procurement form for PO routing. Happy to hop on a quick call to walk through the numbers.

Thanks,
Kyle
Kyle Anders
Head of Infrastructure Operations
ClearBridge AI

Attachments: procurement_form.pdf


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Kyle Anders <kyle.anders@clearbridge.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Feb 2027 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Retroactive capacity charge — Jan spike and renewal

Hi Kyle — thanks for the heads up and the procurement form. We pulled the usage breakdown and reconciliation for Jan; I've attached the CSV (usage_jan2027.csv) and a short mutual action plan (mutual_action_plan.docx) that outlines next steps.

Summary (high level):
- Committed capacity for Jan: 2.0M token units
- Actual billed consumption: 2.84M token units
- Delta (overage): 0.84M token units (~42% over)
- Unit rate (post-renewal committed pricing): $0.0022/token unit
- One-time gross charge: $1,848 (0.84M * $0.0022) — note: this is the usage-based line only, separate from any support or professional fees.

Proposed options for settlement:
1) One-time invoice for $1,848, payable Net 30.
2) Amortize the charge over 3 months (3 x $616).
3) Hold 50% ($924) in an escrow/verification account for 60 days while ClearBridge validates audit logs; remaining 50% due on Net 30.

If option 3 is preferred, Ben (CC) will issue an invoice with an escrow flag and provide payment instructions. To proceed we'll need the PO number from your procurement form and confirmation of the ledger contact for remittance.

Happy to jump on a 20-minute call to finalize.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123

Attachments: usage_jan2027.csv; mutual_action_plan.docx

Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Feb 2027 14:28:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Retroactive capacity charge — invoice and escrow details

Kyle — looping in finance details and invoice approach. Per Aditya's reconciliation, we can implement option 3 (escrow) or option 2 (amortize). Here is the formal invoice stub:

Invoice: INV-CB-4321
Account: ClearBridge AI
Period: Jan 2027 overage reconciliation
Line 1 — Usage overage: 840,000 token units @ $0.0022 = $1,848.00
Line 2 — Billing processing fee = $0 (waived)
Total due = $1,848.00

Payment options:
- Standard: full payment Net 30 to Redwood account (wire/ACH).
- Escrow: we can place 50% hold (default) in our escrow custody and mark invoice accordingly; the remaining 50% will be due Net 30. Escrow release requires confirmation of audit evidence (logs and reconciliation sheet).

If you want escrow at a different percentage or a different amortization schedule, we can accommodate but need the PO and an approved SOW amendment for internal accounting. Once we have the PO, I will: 1) issue the docusign invoice link, 2) generate an internal GL ticket and 3) provision the escrow flag in our billing system.

DocuSign invoice (draft): https://docusign.com/tx/abcdef123456
HubSpot record: https://app.redwood.com/hubspot/contacts/CB-4321

Thanks — Ben Carter

Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 10:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Retroactive capacity charge — invoice and escrow details

Thanks Ben and Aditya. Couple quick asks from our side after review with finance:
1) We'd like to split the payment across 3 months (amortize) to smooth cashflow — essentially Option 2 above (3 x $616).
2) For escrow, 30% holdback is more typical for us for short reconciliation windows — can Redwood set escrow at 30% instead of 50%?
3) We will provide PO# CB-PO-2027-0891; attached our signed procurement_form.pdf.
4) As evidence for escrow release, we will need the detailed audit logs you referenced and confirmation of SOC 2 controls (summary). Can you share a link to the logs folder (read-only) and a SOC 2 summary?

If Redwood can accept 30% escrow and the 3-month amortization, we will review the DocuSign and route the first payment immediately.

Cheers,
Kyle
